subject: Gaming
title: Intrinsic vs Extrinsic Motivation
description: A quick win: understand Intrinsic vs Extrinsic Motivation.

hook.text: Some people play for the joy of it. Others play for rewards. The difference changes how games are designed.

buildup.text: Intrinsic motivation means playing because it's fun. Extrinsic motivation means playing for trophies.

discovery.text: Research shows that adding extrinsic rewards can actually reduce intrinsic enjoyment—the overjustification effect.

twist.text: Achievements and trophies exploit this. Players grind for points instead of enjoying the game itself.

climax.text: The best games balance both: fun mechanics that feel rewarding even without external validation.

punchline.text: Add too many trophies and fun disappears. Rewards can kill joy.

quiz.question: What is the overjustification effect in gaming?
options: External rewards can reduce the intrinsic enjoyment of playing
Players become more motivated with more rewards
Games become harder with more achievements
